Exit 2 on 2026-06-16T11:29:10 from broad docs structure rules. The audit reports zero broken links. Remaining failures are repo-wide docs framework debt: missing foundation split files, missing 00-overview.md in docs/30-plans and docs/20-implementation/99-appendix, plan files without frontmatter, and naming warnings for preserved HTML/assets and unnumbered implementation subdirectories.

fixes_after_handoff: Fast run-evidence refresh now refreshes priorities and refills only from the active epoch's admitted target set; it no longer calls the admission helper that can add new out-of-epoch work.
Existing queued/leased target adoption now excludes only targets already present in the current epoch, so queued repair/carry-forward rows from a closed epoch can be adopted by the next epoch.
The implementation overview appendix link now points at the existing Pi agent run report document.

The primary CLI entrypoint for the deterministic scheduler is now run-loop. trigger-agent and bootstrap remain aliases, run-loop results use mode run_loop, and babysit defaults its child system command to run-loop.
allowed_residue: The legacy director_cycles SQLite table and status/dashboard read paths remain for old state readability; current scheduling does not write it.
Historical objective bundles and preserved HTML design artifacts still mention the old director model.
The scheduler-loop markdown file keeps the historical filename docs/10-system-design/10-run-director-loop.md while its title/content describe the current deterministic scheduler.
The run-loop implementation still lives in apps/cli/src/cli/commands/trigger-agent.ts so existing imports can remain stable while the CLI command surface moves first.
residual_risks: Docs quick audit remains nonzero because of broad docs-structure debt outside this scheduler objective.
Full boundary maintenance still depends on the project report/build toolchain being available in live non-dry-run environments.
